mnemonics: VC Dimension: Very Complicated Dimension. V = Variables, C = Complexity. It's the number of points a model can shatter (correctly classify) in all possible ways, measuring its capacity.
keyPoints: The VC dimension is a measure of the capacity of a model, defined as the maximum number of points that can be shattered (classified in all possible ways) by the model.
A model with a higher VC dimension has a higher capacity and can represent more complex functions, but is also more prone to overfitting.
The number of parameters is not always equivalent to the VC dimension; some models with few parameters can have a very high or even infinite VC dimension (e.g., a 1-nearest neighbor classifier).
commonMistakes: Assuming that a higher number of parameters always means a higher VC dimension. While often correlated, the relationship is not always direct.
Believing that a higher VC dimension is always better. A model with too high a VC dimension for the amount of available data will overfit the training set.
